circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: This 5. 281-kilometer track on Yas Island was designed by renowned circuit architect Hermann Tilke and has been the venue for Formula One's season finale since 2009. The facility represents the second Middle Eastern Formula One circuit to be constructed, following Bahrain's circuit and preceding later additions in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: The Albert Park Circuit is a street-based motorsport venue located around a lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb, hosting the annual Formula One Australian Grand Prix along with domestic and supporting racing categories. The facility maintains the highest FIA safety and operational classification.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km motorsport circuit originally opened as the Österreichring and served as the Austrian Grand Prix venue for 18 straight years beginning in 1970. Following a redesign and shortening, the track was renamed and later returned to hosting the Austrian Grand Prix from 1997 to 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street racing venue in Azerbaijan's capital city, positioned along Baku Boulevard with a lap distance of 6. 003 km, ranking it as the fourth-longest track in Formula One. The circuit debuted in motorsport in 2016 hosting the European Grand Prix, before becoming home to the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onward under the management of Baku City Circuit Operation Company.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the Bahrain International Circuit is a 5. 412 km motorsport facility that hosted the Middle East's inaugural Formula One Grand Prix and continues to serve as the venue for the annual F1 race alongside other competitions. The track has accommodated various racing series including GP2, drag racing, and Australian V8 Supercars (which competed there from 2006 until 2010), while also hosting 24-hour endurance events, and holds FIA Grade 1 certification with multiple configurable layouts.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this 4. 657 km Spanish motorsport facility features a balanced design combining lengthy straightaways with diverse corner configurations, making it suitable for testing multiple racing disciplines. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its stands.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: Located in the Walloon region of Belgium near the town of Spa, this 7-kilometer circuit has served as the home of the Belgian Grand Prix since 1925 and participated in Formula One's inaugural 1950 season. The venue has maintained an annual Grand Prix presence from 1985 onward, with only two exceptions in 2003 and 2006.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: A 4. 361-kilometer racing facility located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal hosts the annual Formula One Canadian Grand Prix and serves as home to Formula One's only street circuit in North America. Beyond its primary role in motorsport's premier series, the venue has accommodated numerous other racing competitions including major sportscar championships and North American open-wheel and stock car series throughout its history.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: Located in Shanghai's Jiading District, the Shanghai International Circuit serves as a premier motorsport venue that hosted the Chinese Grand Prix annually between 2004 and 2019, resuming from 2024. The track has expanded its racing calendar to include electric racing championships beginning in 2024 alongside the Formula One event.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Silverstone Circuit, situated in Northamptonshire, England, has served as the venue for the British Grand Prix since its inaugural hosting in 1948 and notably staged the first-ever World Championship race in 1950. After alternating with other circuits during the mid-20th century, the Grand Prix became a permanent fixture at Silverstone from 1987 onward, while the track continues to welcome international motorcycle racing competitions as well.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring, located near Budapest in Hungary, hosted the first Formula One Grand Prix held in Eastern Europe when it debuted in 1986. Originally, plans called for a street circuit modeled after Monaco within Budapest's central park, but authorities instead constructed the permanent facility outside the city in just eight months, making it the fastest-built Formula One circuit ever.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: Located near Monza north of Milan, this 5. 793 km Italian circuit ranks as the world's third purpose-built racing venue when it opened in 1922 and remains mainland Europe's oldest. The venue has continuously hosted the Italian Grand Prix since 1949, except for a 1980 closure during renovation, earning its nickname as a "Temple of Speed" from its notably long straights and fast-paced corners.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit, situated in Mie Prefecture, Japan, spans 5. 807 kilometers and serves as a premier motorsport venue operated by Honda Mobilityland. The track, which accommodates 155,000 spectators, hosts prestigious international racing competitions including Formula One and Japanese Super Formula championships.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street track routing through Paradise, Nevada, that follows major thoroughfares lined with the city's prominent hospitality venues and entertainment destinations. The course incorporates iconic locations such as MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas as it winds through the urban landscape.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Mexico City's 4. 304 km motorsport circuit honors brothers Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, both fatally injured in racing accidents during the 1960s. The track has intermittently hosted Formula One's Mexican Grand Prix, most recently from 2015 onward under its current Mexico City Grand Prix designation, after a 23-year absence from the calendar.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: A temporary circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens winds through 19 corners across 3. 363 miles, with qualifying speeds averaging approximately 139 mph. Designed by specialized Formula One circuit architects, the track debuted as the venue for Miami's inaugural Grand Prix entry in the 2022 F1 season.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco, a 3. 337 km street course running through Monte Carlo and La Condamine districts along Monaco's harbor, is officially known as the Monte Carlo circuit due to the majority of its layout occupying the Monte Carlo area.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: The Mascot Circuit Zandvoort, a 4. 259 km motorsport venue situated in the Dutch coastal dunes northwest of Amsterdam, hosted the revived Dutch Grand Prix starting in 2021 before their Formula One partnership concludes in 2026. Originally called Circuit Park Zandvoort until 2017, the track has operated under its current sponsorship name since mid-2025.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: Located north of Doha in Qatar, the Lusail International Circuit is a 5. 419-kilometer racing venue featuring a 1. 068-kilometer main straight and holding FIA Grade 1 certification.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: This São Paulo motorsport venue, originally opened in 1940, was renamed in 1985 to commemorate the late Formula 1 driver José Carlos Pace, who perished in an aviation accident eight years earlier. The 4. 309 km track operates in a counterclockwise direction and houses a karting facility dedicated to fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: A street racing venue in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, the Corniche Circuit has hosted Formula One's Saudi Arabian Grand Prix since 2021 and began accommodating Formula E competition in 2025. Situated along Jeddah's waterfront promenade overlooking the Red Sea, the circuit serves as a venue for both racing series.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit is a street racing course that winds through the Marina Bay area of Singapore, covering portions of the Downtown Core and Kallang districts.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this 4. 657 km Spanish motorsport venue features a balanced design combining extended straights with diverse corner configurations, making it suitable for testing multiple racing disciplines. The facility holds FIA Grade 1 certification and can accommodate approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas spans 3. 426 miles and meets the highest FIA safety standards for international motorsport competition. The venue currently hosts major racing series including Formula One's United States Grand Prix, NASCAR events, and MotoGP's Americas round, along with the prestigious Lone Star Le Mans endurance race.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a Thai-British motorsport competitor who drives for Williams in Formula One, representing Thailand in the sport.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso currently races for Aston Martin in Formula One, having secured back-to-back world championships with Renault in 2005 and 2006 alongside 32 career Grand Prix victories. Beyond single-seater racing, Alonso achieved major success in endurance competition, capturing the 2018–19 World Endurance Championship, two Le Mans 24 Hours victories with Toyota, and the 2019 Daytona 24 Hours, making him the sole competitor to win both the F1 drivers' title and the World Endurance Championship.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli made his Formula One debut in 2025 with Mercedes, where he has secured five race victories.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman represents a British talent competing at motorsport's highest level, currently driving for the Haas team in Formula One.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian driver Gabriel Bortoleto currently races in Formula One as a member of the Audi team.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas competes in Formula One for Cadillac and has accumulated 10 Grand Prix victories throughout his 13-season career. He achieved second-place finishes in the World Drivers' Championship during 2019 and 2020 while racing for Mercedes.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Argentine Formula One driver Franco Alejandro Colapinto currently races for the Alpine team in the sport's premier motorsport competition.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Jack Doohan is an Australian racing driver competing in Europe's premier endurance racing series with Nielsen Racing in the LMP2 category. He holds a reserve driver position with the Haas Formula One team and made appearances at seven Grand Prix events during the 2024-2025 season.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: A French Formula One driver, Gasly currently races for Alpine and achieved a notable victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix while competing for AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar is a dual French-Algerian driver currently competing in Formula One with Red Bull Racing, representing France in the sport.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: A British Formula One driver competing for Ferrari, Hamilton shares the record for most world championship titles with seven victories, matching Michael Schumacher's tally. He also leads in multiple statistical categories including race victories, pole positions, and podium appearances throughout the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: German driver Nicolas Hülkenberg currently competes in Formula One for Audi and has also achieved success in endurance racing, most notably securing victory at the 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 while driving for Porsche.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: A Formula One driver from Monaco, Leclerc races for Ferrari and has secured eight Grand Prix victories over his nine-season career. He came closest to the world championship in 2022, finishing second in the drivers' standings while competing for the Italian team.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Danish driver Kevin Magnussen has competed across multiple racing series, including a stint in Formula One that spanned from 2014 to 2020 and again from 2022 to 2024. Currently, he races full-time in the FIA World Endurance Championship for BMW through WRT and competes part-time in NASCAR's top series with Trackhouse Racing.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: A British driver competing for McLaren in Formula One, Norris claimed the drivers' championship in 2025 and has secured 11 race victories during his eight-season tenure in the sport.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon, a French racing driver currently competing in Formula One for Haas, achieved a notable victory at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix while driving for Alpine. This remains a significant highlight in his racing career.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Sergio Pérez, a Mexican Formula One driver currently racing for Cadillac, finished second in the 2023 drivers' championship while competing for Red Bull. Throughout his 15-year career in the sport, he has achieved six Grand Prix victories.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Australian driver Oscar Piastri currently races for McLaren in Formula One, where he has accumulated nine race victories over his four-year tenure in the sport.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: An Australian former Formula One driver, Daniel Joseph Ricciardo competed in the sport between 2011 and 2024, earning eight Grand Prix victories during his 14-season career. Known by the nickname "the Honey Badger," Ricciardo established himself as a consistent race-winning competitor throughout his time in the championship.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: A British Formula One driver for Mercedes, Russell has secured seven Grand Prix victories during his eight-season career in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz Vázquez de Castro has secured four Formula One victories during his 12-season career in the sport, currently racing for Williams.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: American driver Logan Hunter Sargeant competed in Formula One during 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to endurance racing. He has raced in the IMSA SportsCar Championship's LMP2 category for Era Motorsport and currently competes in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 class with Proton Competition.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian Formula One driver currently racing for Aston Martin in the sport's premier motorseries.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Japanese driver Yuki Tsunoda held a reserve position with Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls while maintaining an active Formula One career spanning from 2021 through 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: A Dutch-Belgian driver competing for Red Bull Racing, Verstappen has claimed four consecutive Formula One World Drivers' Championship titles between 2021 and 2024, while accumulating 71 Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu represents China as a racing driver currently serving as reserve driver for Cadillac in Formula One. He competed in the sport's top tier from 2022 through 2024, holding the distinction of being the sole Chinese national to have raced in Formula One competition.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based Formula One team has operated under the Alpine Racing name since 2021, following its rebranding from Renault F1 Team to highlight the French manufacturer's Alpine sports car division. The squad, which competed as Renault's factory team until the automaker's 2025 withdrawal, achieved its only Alpine-era victory through Esteban Ocon at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix and currently competes with Mercedes power units.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin, a British automotive manufacturer, has competed in Formula One through multiple team entities, with its current incarnation operating as a works team in partnership with a Saudi Arabian energy company.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of Italian luxury carmaker Ferrari, is F1's longest-standing team and has participated in every World Championship since the sport's inception in 1950. Known colloquially as "the Prancing Horse" after their iconic emblem, the squad stands as the most accomplished outfit in the sport's history.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: American businessman Gene Haas founded this Formula One outfit in 2014, with the squad entering competition starting in 2016. Leadership transitioned in early 2024 when Ayao Komatsu took over as team principal from the departing Guenther Steiner, who had held the position since the team's establishment.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: A Swiss motorsport manufacturer founded in 1970 by Peter Sauber, the company built both sports and Formula One cars, winning two world championships in endurance racing including the 1989 Le Mans 24 Hours. After competing in Formula One from 1993 until 2025, it became the fourth-oldest constructor by race entries before being acquired by Audi AG to serve as the technical foundation for the Audi F1 Team.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing Limited is a British motorsport outfit headquartered at its technology facility in Woking, Surrey, competing in Formula One under its current commercial partnership designation. The team operates as part of the McLaren Group and is fully owned by Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: The German automotive manufacturer Mercedes-Benz has maintained a presence in Formula One since 1954 through team ownership and engine supply roles. The current Mercedes-AMG Petronas operation, based in Brackley and holding a German licence, became jointly owned by Mercedes-Benz Group, Toto Wolff, and Ineos in equal thirds following an announcement in December 2020. Mercedes teams are traditionally nicknamed the "Silver Arrows".

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team that began competing in 2024, based in Faenza with an additional facility near Milton Keynes. As one of two F1 constructors owned by Austrian conglomerate Red Bull GmbH, it operates alongside Red Bull Racing as part of the same corporate structure.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One constructor that began competing in the 2024 season, operating as one of two teams under Red Bull GmbH's ownership alongside Red Bull Racing. Headquartered in Faenza with a secondary facility near its sister team in Milton Keynes, the squad represents Red Bull's expanded presence in the sport.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing is an Austrian-licensed Formula One team headquartered in England and owned by the Red Bull beverage conglomerate, which also operates a second F1 team called Racing Bulls. Christian Horner led the team from its 2005 inception through 2024, after which Laurent Mekies assumed the leadership role.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering Limited, which will compete under the Atlassian Williams F1 Team banner in 2026, represents a British Formula One constructor established by Frank Williams and Patrick Head in 1977, succeeding Williams's prior unsuccessful racing venture. The organization operates from a 60-acre facility located in Grove, Oxfordshire.
